task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Units of Measure' setting is enabled in the configuration.

task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the Units of Measure list or form views showing 'Case of 6' and 'Case of 12' exist, and their category is explicitly set to 'Unit'.

task_completion.description: Verify the draft Purchase Order for 'Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits' is updated with the correct quantities and new units of measure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the Purchase Order showing Hendrick's Gin with a quantity of 3 and UoM 'Case of 6', and Woodford Reserve with a quantity of 5 and UoM 'Case of 12'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Purchase Order has been confirmed.

task_completion.description: Verify the incoming receipt associated with the Purchase Order has been validated.

task_completion.description: Verify the inventory reflects the correct converted bottle counts for both products.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the Products list, Inventory Valuation, or Product form showing exactly 18 units on hand for Hendrick's Gin 750ml and 60 units on hand for Woodford Reserve Bourbon 750ml.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the tasks rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web interface being interacted with (clicks, form fills, menu navigation) rather than a terminal window executing scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent followed the actual procurement workflow rather than manually adjusting inventory quantities.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the Purchase Order being confirmed and the Receipt being validated, with no evidence of the agent using the 'Update Quantity' button on the product page to manually force the final stock numbers.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the records and updated the system rather than altering the DOM/HTML to appear correct.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the natural flow of opening forms, typing values, and saving, rather than sudden unexplained state changes or the browser's developer tools being open.
